first real pass — single-tile, dim=-1, numeric_stable=True. Now expanding coverage.

Changed scaler CB from bf16 to fp32; deviations stayed ~2e-3 (atol=1e-3). Only 2 cases moved 14->12. The bf16 scaler was not the primary issue. PCC=0.999 still passes — values are correctly proportional but off-scale. Suspect recip precision or DEST/multiply-path precision.
hook_misreport: hook claimed PASS but exit was 1

note: All 44 cases (24 acceptance + 4 compute_kernel_config + 16 validation) PASS. Root cause of the 12-fail residue: recip_tile defaulted to legacy_compat=true which lost ~10-11 bits of precision. recip_tile<legacy_compat=false> uses the newer Newton-Raphson path with claim of <=1 ulp at fp32 precision, fixing every sum-to-1 deviation.
Production timing run — no race conditions, no hangs. Same 44/44 pass as the --dev run.
